auditVerdict.reference: Player control verified, client seed changes 84.3% of outcomes

overview.title: Plinko Audit Overview
overview.intro: This audit independently validates the Plinko game operated by Duel.com across five domains: deterministic outcome generation, entropy integrity, live-to-verifier parity, RTP mathematical accuracy, and fairness integrity testing.
whatWasAudited: The RNG algorithm is deterministic and independently verifiable
Server seeds are cryptographically committed before play
Players can set and change their own client seeds
Nonces increment correctly and are not reused within the verified dataset
Each row decision is derived from HMAC-SHA256 output
Slot landing positions are reproducible from server seed, client seed, and nonce
Payout logic matches the published multiplier configuration for all 27 configurations
Theoretical RTP is 99.9% across all configurations at the tested bet amounts
Bet amount does not alter the slot RNG path
Players can independently verify every tested bet

rows._comment: More tables can be added by adding another object
rows.description: Server seed hashing, timing, reveal mechanics
Client seed control, nonce lifecycle, epoch rotation
HMAC-SHA256 algorithm verification, bias testing, serial independence
Multiplier table accuracy, scaling edge structure, bet-size equivalence
Independent slot recomputation vs live game results
Analytical proof, simulated RTP, chi-squared goodness-of-fit
Game-level integrity checks under adversarial conditions
Game-level integrity checks under adversarial conditions

guarantees: Outcomes are deterministic and reproducible from the recorded inputs
Live game results match independent recomputation for the verified sample (7,600 / 7,600)
Slot distribution follows the expected binomial B(n, 0.5)
RTP is proven analytically: independent binomial probabilities × observed multiplier table = 99.900%
Client seed changes materially influence results
Published payout logic is consistent with the tested output set
All fairness integrity checks passed at audit time
excludes: Bet-size brackets above bracket 0 (bets above ~$335 face higher house edge — documented, not tested)

note.strong: All code, datasets, and simulation scripts are publicly accessible.
note.body: Anyone can independently verify every finding in this audit.

references.title: Plinko — Game Rules
references.intro: Plinko is a binary-path game where a ball drops through a triangular grid of pegs. At each peg, the ball bounces left or right. The number of rows is player-configurable: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, or 16. Its transparent mechanics and verifiable math make it ideal for provably fair auditing.

sections.title: Game Rules
sections.content: Plinko is a binary-path game where players bet on where a ball will land after bouncing through a grid of pegs. The game offers complete control over risk and reward: players who choose high-risk multiplier tables accept more volatile payouts in exchange for higher potential wins, while those preferring low-risk tables receive smoother, more predictable returns.

sections.title: How to Play
steps: Choose row count — Select between 8 and 16 rows. More rows means more pegs, more possible slots, and more extreme edge payouts.
Choose risk level — Select Low, Medium, or High risk. This determines the multiplier table applied to each slot. Risk level does not affect the ball path.
Enter bet amount — Choose how much to wager.
Drop — Click the drop button. The ball falls through the peg grid, bouncing left or right at each row.
Outcome — The ball lands in a slot (0 to N). Your payout is bet_amount × multiplier for that slot.
sections.note: The game can be played in Manual mode (one drop at a time) or Auto mode (automated sequential drops with configurable parameters).

sections.title: Win Conditions
sections.content: The win condition in Plinko is determined by the landing slot and its corresponding multiplier.

rows: Ball lands on a 1.0× slot → No gain/loss

sections.note: Important: Unlike Dice where you can lose 100% on a single bet, Plinko always returns something — every slot has a multiplier > 0. However, on High risk, center slots pay as low as 0.20×, meaning you lose 80% of your bet on the most probable outcomes. The expected return across all slots is 99.9% (bracket 0).

sections.title: Risk vs Reward
sections.content: The core mechanic of Plinko is the tradeoff between risk level and payout distribution.
bullets: High risk, high reward: Selecting 16 rows on High risk means center slots pay only 0.2× but edge slots pay up to 1009.33×
Low risk, low reward: Selecting 8 rows on Low risk means most slots pay near 1× with edge slots paying up to 5.6×
Players control the math: Unlike slots or other games with fixed odds, Plinko lets players choose their exact risk profile on every bet via row count and risk level

rows: Affects multiplier table only, not ball path
N = number of rows. Slot = count of right-turns
Progressive: scales to 2% at highest stakes

sections.title: Seed Formats
sections.content: Every Plinko bet uses three cryptographic inputs to generate the result.
sections.note: The combination of these three inputs, using HMAC-SHA256, produces the ball path. Because the player controls the client seed and the server seed is committed before betting, neither party can manipulate the outcome.

sections.title: Multiplier Calculation
sections.content: Payouts in Plinko are determined by a multiplier table that maps each landing slot to a payout ratio. The tables are pre-configured per (rows, risk level) combination. Multipliers are set so that: Expected RTP = Σ [ P(slot) × multiplier(slot) ] = 99.9%. With Duel's 0.1% house edge at bracket 0, the slot probabilities follow the binomial distribution B(N, 0.5) and multipliers are calibrated to produce a theoretical RTP of exactly 99.9% across all 27 configurations.

sections.title: Example: 16 Rows, High Risk
sections.note: Edge slots (0 and 16) have a probability of approximately 1 in 65,536. They will not appear in most data captures of a few thousand bets.

references.title: Why Provably Fair Matters
references.intro: Traditional online casinos require players to trust that games are fair. Provably fair systems eliminate this trust requirement by allowing players to mathematically verify that outcomes were not manipulated. In a Provably Fair system:
bullets: The casino commits to a result before the player bets
The player contributes randomness that the casino cannot predict
Anyone can verify the outcome after the fact

sections.title: Checklist Reference
sections.intro: Based on the ProvablyFair.org Audit Execution Checklist, here are the tests that are covered under this audit document.

rows: Server seed commit exists before play
Hash shown to player before betting
Starts at 0, +1 per bet, never reused
RNG depends only on seeds + nonce
RNG → game range has equal distribution

sections.title: High-Level Flow
sections.content: To get an overview of how the process works, here is a high-level breakdown:
steps: Player Bets — Selects rows (8–16), risk level (low/medium/high), and bet amount
Seeds Combined — Combining serverSeed, clientSeed, and nonce
RNG Output — For each row (cursor 0 to rows-1): HMAC-SHA256(hexDecode(serverSeed), clientSeed:nonce:cursor), first 4 bytes as uint32, mod 2
Game Logic — Each row produces left (0) or right (1). Final slot = total right-turns across all rows
Payout Result — win_amount = bet_amount × multiplier[slot] from the configured multiplier table

terms.definition: A cryptographic system that allows players to mathematically verify that game outcomes were not manipulated. Unlike traditional "trust-based" systems, provably fair games provide cryptographic proof of fairness.

A two-phase process in which the casino commits to a result (by showing its hash) before the player bets, then reveals the actual value after the bet. This prevents the casino from changing outcomes based on player actions.
The property that identical inputs always produce identical outputs. In provably fair systems, using the same server seed, client seed, and nonce must always generate the same game result.
A random value generated by the casino, typically 64 hexadecimal characters (32 bytes). The server seed is hashed and shown to players before betting, then revealed after the bet is complete.

A random value controlled by the player, typically 16 alphanumeric characters. Players can set or change their client seed at any time to ensure they contribute entropy that the casino cannot predict.
A sequential counter (0, 1, 2, 3...) that increments with each bet under the same seed pair. The nonce ensures each bet produces a unique result even when using the same server and client seeds.

The combination of a server seed and a client seed. A seed pair remains active across multiple bets, with the nonce incrementing for each round. When a player rotates seeds, a new seed pair begins with the nonce reset to 0.

The SHA-256 hash of the server seed, shown to players before betting. After the bet, players can verify that SHA-256(revealed server seed) equals the originally shown hash, proving the casino didn't change the seed.

Hash-based Message Authentication Code using SHA-256. A cryptographic function that combines the server seed, client seed, and nonce to produce a deterministic, unpredictable hash used for random number generation.
Secure Hash Algorithm 256-bit. A one-way cryptographic hash function that converts any input into a unique 64-character hexadecimal output. Cannot be reversed to discover the original input.

In Plinko, each row's left/right decision is determined by taking the first 4 bytes of the HMAC output as a uint32 integer, then computing mod 2. Because 2³² mod 2 = 0, there is zero modulo bias — left and right are exactly equiprobable.
A tool (usually web-based or code snippet) that independently calculates game outcomes using provided seeds and nonce. A proper verifier should produce identical results to the live game.
The degree of matching between verifier results and live game results. 100% parity means every single outcome matches perfectly, which is required for provably fair certification.
The ability to regenerate exact game outcomes using the same inputs. Players should be able to reproduce any historical bet result using the revealed seeds and nonce.
The algorithm that produces random outcomes. In provably fair systems, the RNG must be deterministic and based only on seeds + nonce (no hidden entropy sources).
The percentage of wagered money returned to players over time. Calculated as (1 - House Edge) × 100%. For Duel Plinko, RTP = 99.9% at bracket 0.

The casino's mathematical advantage, expressed as a percentage of each bet. For Duel Plinko, house edge = 0.1% at bracket 0, meaning the casino expects to keep 0.1% of all wagers long-term. Higher bet brackets have progressively higher house edges up to 2%.

The payout ratio for a given landing slot. In Plinko, multipliers are pre-set per (rows, risk level) configuration. For example, slot 0 on 16-row High risk pays 1009.33×, while center slot 8 pays 0.20×.

The final landing position of the ball, determined by the total number of right-turns across all rows. For N rows, slots range from 0 (all left) to N (all right). Slot probabilities follow the binomial distribution B(N, 0.5).

The probability distribution describing the number of right-turns in N independent rows, each with probability 0.5. The probability of landing in slot k out of N rows is C(N,k) × 0.5^N. Center slots are most probable; edge slots are exponentially rare.
A condition where a player or casino could predict, alter, or unfairly influence outcomes. Examples include seed prediction, nonce replay, or hidden entropy injection.
Randomness or unpredictability in a system. In provably fair games, entropy comes from both the casino (server seed) and player (client seed).
Non-uniform probability distribution. An unbiased RNG gives all outcomes equal likelihood. In Plinko, mod 2 on a 32-bit integer produces exactly zero bias for left/right decisions.
Unusual or extreme scenarios that might behave differently than normal operation. Examples: concurrent betting, nonce overflow, malformed seeds, or maximum bet limits.
Base-16 number system using digits 0-9 and letters A-F. Server seeds and hashes are typically shown in hex format (e.g., 4f775f81301c7fe8...).
The output of a cryptographic hash function. In provably fair systems, hashes serve as commitments that cannot be reversed but can be verified.
A unique identifier assigned to each bet (e.g., #308953686). Used to reference specific rounds during verification.

sections.title: Seed, Nonce & Determinism
sections.subtitle: Can the casino change your outcome after you bet?
sections.intro: Every Plinko drop on Duel is generated from three inputs: server seed, client seed, and nonce. The casino commits to its seed before you bet, you control your own seed, and the nonce increments automatically. At each row, these inputs are combined via HMAC-SHA256 to produce a single left/right decision — the ball's final slot is the sum of all right-turns across every row.

playerBrief.headline: Commit-Reveal Cryptographic Guarantee
verified: Casino commits to server seed before any bet
Players can set or change their client seed
Each row's L/R derived from HMAC-SHA256(serverSeed, clientSeed:nonce:cursor)
Same inputs always produce same slot position
Client seed is a verified HMAC input — 84.3% of slots differ when recomputed with an alternate seed
means: Casino cannot change outcomes after you bet
You contribute your own randomness via client seed
Every bet is unique, even with the same seeds
Any Plinko result can be verified independently
Outcomes are tamper-proof, even months later

verdict.title: Commit-reveal verified
verdict.body: The audited seed lifecycle matched the expected commit → play → reveal model, with no evidence of post-bet seed substitution in the verified dataset.

howItWorks.title: Seed lifecycle
howItWorks.content: A server seed is generated and hashed before use. The hash acts as the public commitment. After the seed rotates, the raw seed is revealed so anyone can hash it and compare it to the earlier commitment.

howItWorks.title: Why epoch consistency matters
howItWorks.content: If multiple distinct commitment hashes appeared inside one seed epoch, that would indicate mid-session seed substitution. The audit checks for that condition and treats it as a failure mode.

howItWorks.title: Client seed influence
howItWorks.content: Changing the client seed while holding the same server seed and nonce should often change the computed path. Some overlap is still expected because identical left/right choices can occur by chance.

howItWorks.title: Nonce progression
howItWorks.content: Nonce values provide deterministic ordering. The nonce begins at 0 and increments by 1 for each bet under the same seed pair, ensuring every bet produces a unique RNG input. The nonce resets to 0 only when the player rotates their client seed.

code.label: tests/verify.ts · Step 2: EC-2
lines: const first = phaseBets.sort((a, b) => a.response.id - b.response.id)[0];

note.body: All 3 pre-capture commitments match the first bet response hash. Server did not switch seeds between commitment and first bet.
152 / 152 seed pairs — server_seed_hashed constant within each. No mid-session seed switches.

code.label: tests/verify.ts · Step 6: EC-27
lines: const correctSlot = computeSlot(serverSeed, client_seed, nonce, rows);
const wrongSlot   = computeSlot(serverSeed, wrongClientSeed, nonce, rows);

note.body: Recomputing with an alternate client seed produced different slots in 6,409 / 7,600 bets (84.3%) across 152 epochs. Per-epoch divergence ranged from 37/50 to 50/50. The 15.7% overlap is expected — each row has a 50% chance of coincidental match.

sectionVerdictTable.finding: Casino cannot change randomness after betting
Player contributes entropy via custom client seed
Sequential nonce from 0, never resets until seed rotation
server_seed_hashed constant across all 152 seed pairs
Same inputs always produce same slot position
Alternate client seed produced different slots in 84.3% of bets (6,409/7,600)

expandables.title: How It Works — Seed, Nonce & Determinism

subsections.title: Server Seed Commitment
subsections.content: Before any bet is placed, the casino generates a secret server seed and publicly commits to it by displaying its SHA-256 hash to the player. This cryptographic commitment prevents the casino from changing the seed after seeing player actions. When the player rotates their client seed, the previous server seed is revealed. The auditor verifies it by hashing it and confirming it matches the pre-committed hash.

note.body: All 152 revealed seeds: SHA-256(hexDecode(serverSeed)) = serverSeedHashed. Zero mismatches.

subsections.title: Commitment Linkage (Pre-Bet Verification)
subsections.content: At the start of each phase (A, B, C), the auditor captured the active server_seed_hashed before placing any bets. After the first bet, the hash echoed in the bet response was compared against the pre-capture commitment.

code.label: tests/verify.ts · Step 2: EC-2
lines: const first = phaseBets.sort((a, b) => a.response.id - b.response.id)[0];

notes.body: All 3 pre-capture commitments match the first bet response hash. Server did not switch seeds between commitment and first bet.
Explicit pre-bet snapshots captured at 3 phase boundaries only. For the remaining 149 seed pairs, the commitment hash is first observed in the first bet response.

subsections.title: Hash Consistency Within Epoch
subsections.content: For each of the 152 seed pairs, the server_seed_hashed field was extracted from every bet response and checked for uniqueness. More than one distinct hash within a seed pair would indicate a mid-session seed switch.

note.body: 152 / 152 seed pairs — server_seed_hashed constant within each. No mid-session seed switches.

subsections.title: Player Client Seed Control
subsections.content: Players have full control over their client seed through the Duel UI, allowing them to view, modify, or randomize it at any time before placing bets. The client seed is an input to every HMAC-SHA256 computation — changing it produces a different ball path.

note.body: 152 unique client seeds observed across the dataset. Client seeds are player-controlled and vary across all phases and epochs.

subsections.title: Nonce Incrementation
subsections.content: The nonce begins at 0 and increments by 1 for each bet under the same seed pair, ensuring every bet produces a unique RNG input. The nonce resets to 0 only when the player rotates their client seed.

notes.body: 152 seed pairs checked. 147 have perfectly clean nonce sequences. 5 show a minor gap caused by the auditor's capture script, not the casino.
The gap exists only in the auditor's captured dataset, not in the casino's actual nonce sequence. This is an artifact of data collection, not a game integrity issue.

extraCodes.label: plinko-master.json · nonce sequence
lines: // ...nonce increments to 49, then player rotates seed...
// New server seed issued, nonce resets

subsections.title: Deterministic Mapping
subsections.content: The RNG algorithm is fully deterministic: given the same server seed, client seed, nonce, and row count, it always produces the exact same slot position.

code.label: src/rng.ts · computeSlot
lines: serverSeed: string, clientSeed: string, nonce: number, rows: number

note.body: All 7,600 bets with revealed seeds: HMAC-SHA256 recompute matches final_slot. Zero mismatches.

subsections.title: Client Seed Influence
subsections.content: To confirm the client seed is a genuine input to the HMAC computation, every bet was recomputed using an alternate client seed.

code.label: tests/verify.ts · Step 6: EC-27
lines: const correctSlot = computeSlot(serverSeed, client_seed, nonce, rows);
const wrongSlot   = computeSlot(serverSeed, wrongClientSeed, nonce, rows);

note.body: Different slots in 6,409 / 7,600 bets (84.3%) across 152 epochs. The 15.7% overlap is expected — each row has a 50% chance of coincidental match.

subsections.title: Purpose
subsections.content: This section indexes the technical artifacts used to verify Plinko seed handling, nonce behavior, and determinism. All evidence is reproducible using the linked scripts and datasets.

rows: Steps 1-6: seed commit, commitment linkage, hash consistency, nonce sequencing, slot recomputation, client seed influence
computeSlot — independent HMAC-SHA256 implementation, verifyHash — SHA-256 commit-reveal check
1,070-line browser capture script with instance guard, auto-save, rotation checkpoints

subsections.title: Datasets Used
subsections.content: Primary: results/merged/plinko-master.json

rows: Live Plinko game data from duel.com/plinko

subsections.extraContent: Supplementary: results/plinko-phase-d.json — 500 bets across 10 configs, 11 seed entries. Fields used: serverSeed, serverSeedHashed, clientSeed, nonce, final_slot, rows, risk_level

rows: Pre-capture commitment hash matches first bet response hash (3/3 phases)
server_seed_hashed constant within each seed pair (152/152)
Nonce starts at 0 when a new server seed is issued
Nonce increments by exactly +1 for every bet
Nonce never decrements, skips, or resets within same seed pair
Nonce values never reused under same server + client seed pair
Client seed is a verified HMAC input (84.3% divergence with alternate seed)

subsections.preBlock:
[PASS] Step 1 — Seed Hash Integrity

note.body: Steps 1-6 cover all Seed, Nonce & Determinism checks — all pass.

sections.title: RNG & Entropy Model
sections.subtitle: Is the randomness genuinely random, or could it be rigged?
sections.intro: This section verifies that Duel's Plinko random number generation produces cryptographically sound, unbiased outputs using HMAC-SHA256 with zero modulo bias. Each row's left/right decision is an exact 50/50 coin flip.

playerBrief.headline: Cryptographic Randomness Verification
verified: No hidden or external entropy sources
Slot distributions match binomial B(n, 0.5) across 27M rounds
means: Plinko outcomes are generated fairly and cannot be skewed
Each row has an exact 50/50 chance of left or right
No hidden randomness or server-side tricks influence results
Consecutive outcomes are not correlated

verdict.title: RNG implementation verified
verdict.body: The independent verifier reproduced all tested Plinko outcomes. Zero modulo bias was mathematically proven. Statistical checks were consistent with an unbiased, serially independent process.

howItWorks.title: RNG function implementation
howItWorks.content: The Duel Plinko RNG uses HMAC-SHA256 with deterministic inputs (serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce, cursor). For each row, the algorithm produces a single bit — left (0) or right (1) — by taking the first 4 bytes of the HMAC output as a 32-bit unsigned integer and computing int % 2. Key encoding: the server seed is hex-decoded to a 32-byte Buffer. Message format: clientSeed:nonce:cursor.

howItWorks.title: Why zero modulo bias matters
howItWorks.content: Plinko uses int % 2 to decide left or right. Because 2³² is exactly divisible by 2, every possible uint32 value maps to left or right with exactly equal probability. There is no bias by construction — unlike some games that use larger moduli and require rejection sampling.

howItWorks.title: Why statistical checks are secondary
howItWorks.content: The strongest evidence is deterministic recomputation of live bets. Statistical tests (chi-squared, serial independence) are supportive checks that look for obvious anomalies, not the primary fairness proof.

pins.value: Phase B (2,000 bets) + Phase C (200) — Pass

code.label: src/rng.ts · computeSlot
lines: serverSeed: string, clientSeed: string, nonce: number, rows: number
slot += int % 2;  // odd = right (+1), even = left (+0)

note.body: 7,600 / 7,600 bets were recomputed using only the four declared inputs. If any hidden entropy source existed, recomputation would fail. It does not.

pins.value: chiSquaredTest (pooling + gamma p-values), lag1Autocorrelation, runsTest, binomProb
Monte Carlo simulation — 1M rounds/config, fresh seeds
Steps 5 (slot recomputation / drand absent), 11 (serial independence), 12 (chi-squared on live bets)

sectionVerdictTable.check: RNG derived only from disclosed inputs
sectionVerdictTable.finding: No hidden randomness affects outcomes
No timestamps, external APIs, or server-side state
2 to the 32nd mod 2 = 0 — exact 50/50, no rejection sampling needed
7,600/7,600 bets recomputed correctly without drand
Lag-1 autocorrelation and runs test both pass
27/27 configs pass at alpha=0.01
27/27 pass at Bonferroni-corrected alpha/27

subsections.title: RNG Function Implementation
subsections.content: The Duel Plinko RNG uses HMAC-SHA256 with deterministic inputs (serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce, cursor). For each row, the algorithm produces a single bit — left (0) or right (1) — by taking the first 4 bytes of the HMAC output as a 32-bit unsigned integer and computing int % 2. Key encoding: the server seed is hex-decoded to a 32-byte Buffer, not treated as a UTF-8 string. Message format: clientSeed:nonce:cursor — no other fields including drand appear.

code.label: src/rng.ts · computeSlot
lines: serverSeed: string, clientSeed: string, nonce: number, rows: number

subsections.title: Entropy Sources
subsections.content: All randomness derives exclusively from the deterministic HMAC-SHA256 function combining three cleanly separated inputs: Server Seed (Casino), Client Seed (Player), and Nonce (System). Verified absent: No timestamps, no Math.random(), no external APIs or drand, no server-side mutable state.

code.label: tests/verify.ts · Step 5: EC-6, EC-7
lines: // If ANY hidden entropy existed, recomputation would fail.
const computed = computeSlot(serverSeed, client_seed, nonce, bet.request.rows);

note.body: 7,600/7,600 bets were recomputed using only the four declared inputs. If any hidden entropy source existed, recomputation would fail. It does not.

rows: Base randomness (committed via SHA-256 hash before betting)

subsections.title: Modulo Bias Analysis
subsections.content: Each row uses int % 2 where int is a 32-bit unsigned integer (range 0 to 4,294,967,295). Because 2³² = 4,294,967,296 is exactly divisible by 2, there are exactly 2,147,483,648 even values and 2,147,483,648 odd values. The probability of a right bounce at any row is exactly 1/2. No rejection sampling is required.

note.body: Dice uses int % 10001 which requires rejection sampling to eliminate the 762-value bias from 2 to the 32nd mod 10001. Plinko's int % 2 has zero bias by construction.

subsections.title: RNG Isolation
subsections.content: The computeSlot function is completely stateless — each bet's result depends solely on its unique (serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce, rows) input tuple. There are no class-level variables, no cross-bet memory, and no global state. Different players receive different server seeds, and the nonce ensures every bet under the same seed pair produces a unique HMAC input.

note.body: The computeSlot implementation is a pure function with no class state, no external calls, and no cross-bet memory. The function signature takes all inputs explicitly and returns a value.

subsections.title: Monte Carlo Simulation (27M Rounds)
subsections.content: A 27,000,000-round Monte Carlo simulation (1,000,000 rounds per config, all 27 configs) verified that the algorithm produces the expected binomial slot distribution at scale. Methodology: Fresh random seeds per config. Expected slot counts from independent binomial B(n, 0.5). Chi-squared goodness-of-fit with Cochran pooling. P-values via regularized incomplete gamma function.

code.label: src/simulate.ts · core loop
lines: const slot = computeSlotSync(key, clientSeed, nonce, rows);
const { chi2, df, pValue } = chiSquaredTest(slotCounts, expectedCounts);

note.body: One config (8r/high, p=0.0059) falls below uncorrected alpha=0.01 — a single-run false positive within the expected family-wise error rate for 27 tests (FWER approximately 23.8%).

subsections.title: Chi-Squared on Live Bets
subsections.content: The same chi-squared test was applied to the 7,600 live bets grouped by configuration. Expected counts computed from independent binomial probabilities.

code.label: tests/verify.ts · Step 12: EC-9
lines: const { chi2, df, pValue } = chiSquaredTest(observed, allExpected);

note.body: All 27 configurations pass at alpha=0.01. Weakest: 16r/low (n=200): chi-squared=20.45, df=10, p=0.0252. Under the null hypothesis, the probability of at least one p < 0.05 across 27 tests is approximately 75%, so this is statistically expected.

subsections.title: Serial Independence (Step 11)
subsections.content: Consecutive outcomes were tested for dependence using two methods on Phase B data (2,000 bets, single config — 16 rows, high risk). Lag-1 autocorrelation measures whether consecutive multiplier values are correlated. Runs test counts runs of consecutive wins/losses.

notes.body: r = -0.0089 (threshold ±0.0671), runs z = -0.483, p = 0.629. Both tests pass comfortably. No serial dependence detected.
Phase A sequences bets across 27 configs, creating artificial run patterns. Serial independence is only meaningful for single-config sequences. Phase C (200 bets) is too short for meaningful power.

subsections.title: Purpose
subsections.content: This section indexes the technical artifacts used to verify the Plinko random number generator, its entropy sources, bias properties, distribution fit, serial independence, and probability independence.

rows: chiSquaredTest (pooling + gamma p-values), lag1Autocorrelation, runsTest, binomProb
Monte Carlo simulation — 1M rounds/config, fresh seeds
Steps 5 (slot recomputation / drand absent), 11 (serial independence), 12 (chi-squared on live bets)

subsections.title: Datasets Used
subsections.content: Simulation: outputs/simulation-results.json — 27M rounds (1M per config), chi-squared results, slot counts. Live chi-squared: outputs/chi-squared-results.json — Per-config chi-squared on 7,600 live bets. Serial independence: Phase B data from results/merged/plinko-master.json — 2,000 consecutive 16r/high bets.

subsections.title: Worked Example
subsections.content: Bet ID 60086141 (Phase B, 16 rows, high risk). serverSeed = 99e516ca44345fcf3c749495ba208076170a4fbfcf7160b02a19474318b975e8, clientSeed = d1AcZhAy1QMp7PDa, nonce = 0, rows = 16. Ball path: L R R R L L L L L R L R R L R R → final_slot = 8.

note.body: Matches the API response. Multiplier for 16r/high slot 8: 0.20186622x.

rows: RNG depends only on declared inputs (serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce, cursor)
No hidden or mixed entropy sources
drand is not an input to Plinko RNG
Modulo bias is zero (2³² mod 2 = 0, exact 50/50)
Serial independence: lag-1 autocorrelation within threshold

sections.title: Verifier Parity
sections.subtitle: Does the verifier produce the exact same results as the live game?
sections.intro: This section validates the most critical requirement of any provably fair system: that the independent verifier produces exactly the same outcomes as the live game. A single mismatch would invalidate the entire fairness guarantee.

playerBrief.headline: Live ↔ Verifier Parity
verified: Live Plinko outcomes versus independent verifier recomputation
Payout correctness: win_amount = bet × multiplier for every bet
Deterministic parity across real production bets at $0.01 and $10
means: The verifier is not a simulation — it produces the exact same result
Every bet you play can be independently recomputed
The casino cannot alter outcomes after bets are placed
Bet amount does not influence the outcome or payout table

verdict.title: Live game and verifier fully aligned
verdict.body: All 7,600 live Plinko outcomes matched the independent verifier exactly. Payout math is correct. Multiplier tables are symmetric and consistent across bet sizes.

howItWorks.title: Why parity matters
howItWorks.content: If the verifier produces results that differ from the live game, players cannot trust the verification — the entire provably fair system becomes meaningless. 100% parity is required because even a single discrepancy would indicate either a bug in the verification logic, manipulation in the live game, or an inconsistent RNG implementation.

howItWorks.title: Replay model
howItWorks.content: The verifier loads the recorded live seeds, client seeds, nonces, and configuration values, then recomputes the final slot using an independent implementation. Both systems process identical inputs; outputs must be bit-for-bit identical.

howItWorks.title: Why parity is the strongest evidence
howItWorks.content: If live outcomes and independent recomputation match at scale, that directly links the documented fairness model to the production game rather than to a simplified demo implementation.

subsections.title: Why Parity Matters
subsections.content: If the verifier produces results that differ from the live game, players cannot trust the verification — the entire provably fair system becomes meaningless. 100% parity is required because even a single discrepancy would indicate either a bug in the verification logic, manipulation in the live game, or inconsistent RNG implementation. Players must be able to take the revealed seeds after gameplay, input them into the independent verifier, and receive the exact same slot position they experienced during live play.

subsections.title: Three-Phase Collection Design

rows: Confirm bet amount is not in the RNG

subsections.extraContent: Why three phases: Phase A proves the verifier works across the full configuration space. Phase B provides enough data for meaningful statistical tests. Phase C is the discrimination test — if the server uses a different code path or multiplier table for larger bets, Phase C would detect it.

sections.title: RTP & Payout Logic Validation
sections.subtitle: Does the house edge match what's advertised?
sections.intro: This section validates that Plinko's payout mechanics are mathematically correct and that the 99.9% RTP is proven analytically — not estimated from sample data. The proof is non-circular: probabilities are independently computed from the binomial distribution, not taken from the casino's configuration.

playerBrief.headline: Payout Fairness Verification
verified: Payout formula correct for all 7,600 bets
House edge transparent and consistent across all 27 configs
means: House edge is exactly what's advertised (0.1%)
Payouts cannot be altered after the slot is determined
The RTP proof doesn't rely on trusting the casino
Higher stakes have higher house edge (disclosed)

verdict.title: RTP behaves as advertised
verdict.body: The 99.9% RTP is proven mathematically from independently verified binomial probabilities multiplied by the observed multiplier table. This is a mathematical proof, not a statistical estimate.

howItWorks.title: Payout formula
howItWorks.content: Winning payouts are calculated as bet_amount × multiplier, where the multiplier comes from the configuration's table based on the slot the ball lands in. Every slot has a multiplier — every bet returns some amount.

howItWorks.title: Analytical RTP proof (anti-circularity)
howItWorks.content: The 99.900% theoretical RTP is established via a two-step chain. Step 1: the slot probabilities in plinkoConfig.json exactly equal the independent binomial B(rows, 0.5), verified exhaustively across all 27 configs. Step 2: theoreticalRTP = Σ binomProb(rows, k) × scalingEdgeMultiplier(rows, risk, k). The binomProb() function in stats.ts does NOT reference plinkoConfig.json — making this proof non-circular.

howItWorks.title: Scaling-edge structure
howItWorks.content: Plinko includes stake brackets that scale the house edge. Bracket 0 = 0.1% house edge (99.9% RTP). Bracket 190 = 2.0% house edge. Both test amounts ($0.01 and $10) remained inside bracket 0. The progressive edge is a disclosed feature, not hidden manipulation.

howItWorks.title: Analytical RTP versus live outcomes
howItWorks.content: Plinko live samples are noisy, especially around rare high-multiplier outcomes. The audit therefore treats theoretical RTP and large-scale simulation as the primary evidence and live empirical RTP as a secondary informational view.

code.label: tests/verify.ts · Step 20: EC-33
lines: // Step 1: Verify config probabilities = independent binomial
// Step 2: Cross-check RTP from independent probs × multipliers
independentRTP += binomProb(rows, k) * cfg.scalingEdgeMultiplier(rows, risk, k);
// binomProb() from stats.ts — does NOT reference plinkoConfig.json

note.body: All 27 configs return 99.900%. The proof is non-circular — probabilities are independently computed, not taken from the casino.

sectionVerdictTable.finding: win_amount = amount x multiplier for all 7,600 bets
99.900% across all 27 configs — proven analytically
Independent binomial probabilities match config exactly
27M rounds, average 99.890%, within expected variance
All live payouts match scaling_edge[0] reference table
Bracket 0 = 0.1% edge, both test amounts within bracket
All effective_edge groups use same multiplier table
10 dollar bets use same table as 0.01 dollar bets

subsections.title: Payout Formula (Step 7)
subsections.content: Winning payouts are calculated as bet_amount x multiplier, where the multiplier comes from the configuration table based on the slot the ball lands in. Every slot has a multiplier — every bet returns some amount.

note.body: All 7,600 bets match within 1e-8 tolerance. No undisclosed deductions or rounding errors.

subsections.title: House Edge and RTP Explained
subsections.content: House Edge: The mathematical advantage the casino holds. A 0.1% house edge means for every $100 wagered, the casino retains $0.10. RTP (Return to Player): The percentage of wagered money paid back over time. RTP = 100% - House Edge. For Plinko at bracket 0: RTP = 99.9%. How Plinko RTP is computed: Each of the 27 configs has its own multiplier table. The RTP for each is the sum of P(slot=k) x multiplier(k) for k = 0 to rows, where P(slot=k) follows the binomial distribution B(rows, 0.5).
subsections.preBlock: RTP = sum of P(slot = k) x multiplier(k)    for k = 0 to rows

subsections.title: Analytical RTP Proof (Step 20)
subsections.content: The 99.900% theoretical RTP is established via a two-step chain. Step 1 — Probability independence: The slot probabilities in plinkoConfig.json exactly equal the independent binomial B(rows, 0.5). Verified exhaustively across all 27 configs and all slots. Step 2 — RTP cross-check:
subsections.preBlock: theoreticalRTP = sum of binomProb(rows, k) x scalingEdgeMultiplier(rows, risk, k)

code.label: tests/verify.ts · Step 20: EC-33
lines: // Step 1: Verify config probabilities = independent binomial
// Step 2: Cross-check RTP from independent probs x multipliers
independentRTP += binomProb(rows, k) * cfg.scalingEdgeMultiplier(rows, risk, k);
// binomProb() from stats.ts — does NOT reference plinkoConfig.json

note.body: All 27 configs return 99.900%. The proof is non-circular — probabilities are independently computed, not taken from the casino.

subsections.title: Full RTP Table — All 27 Configurations
subsections.content: Theoretical vs simulated (1M rounds each), bracket 0:

note.body: High-variance configs (e.g., 16r/high) show larger deviations because the 1,009x jackpot at 1/65,536 probability means ~15 hits per 1M rounds — high sensitivity to individual jackpot events.

subsections.title: Empirical RTP from Live Bets (Informational Only)
subsections.content: Per-config empirical RTP is not used as fairness evidence. At n = 116-285 bets per config with multipliers up to 1,009x, no sample-based RTP test has meaningful statistical power.

note.body: Live data proves determinism, parity, and payout formula correctness only.

subsections.title: Scaling Edge Structure (Step 19)
subsections.content: plinkoConfig.json contains a scaling_edge array with 191 bet-size brackets per config. The house edge scales progressively:

note.body: All 27 configs: bracket 0 = 0.001 (0.1%). Both test amounts ($0.01, $10) within bracket 0 ceiling. Progressive edge is a disclosed feature, not manipulation.

subsections.title: Zero Edge Audit (Step 13)
subsections.content: Duel.com displays Zero Edge branding. The verified mathematical house edge is 0.1%, not 0%. Duel returns this 0.1% as instant rakeback — making the effective player experience 0% edge. The rakeback mechanism is a platform-level payment feature outside this cryptographic audit.

note.body: All effective_edge groups use the same multiplier table. No multiplier switching based on edge classification.

subsections.title: Multiplier Structure by Risk Level
subsections.content: All three risk levels target the same RTP (~99.9%) but differ in variance:
subsections.extraContent: Risk level shifts payout weight between center and edge slots. RTP is the same regardless — the tradeoff is pure variance preference.

sections.title: Fairness Integrity Testing
sections.subtitle: Can anyone cheat the system — players or the casino?
sections.intro: This section tests whether any party — player or casino — can predict outcomes, alter results, or gain an unfair advantage through weaknesses in the provably fair implementation. Testing covers 5 fairness guarantee categories with 15 individual tests under realistic and adversarial conditions.

playerBrief.headline: Fairness Integrity Testing
means: No one — not the player, not the casino — can know the outcome before it is determined
Once a bet is placed, the result cannot be changed
Each bet is cryptographically unique and irreversible
Your results are independent of every other player's results

verdict.title: All fairness guarantees verified
verdict.body: All provably fair integrity tests passed. No adversarial condition was able to violate any fairness guarantee under realistic gameplay conditions.

howItWorks.title: Framework overview
howItWorks.content: Testing follows the ProvablyFair.org Fairness Integrity Framework — a structured methodology derived from real, historically observed failures in provably fair systems. Five categories tested independently: Nonce Integrity, Seed Commitment, Outcome Determinism, Round & Player Isolation, and Payout Integrity.

howItWorks.title: Nonce integrity (4 tests)
howItWorks.content: Each bet uses unique, sequential nonce (FI-NONCE-001). Nonce progression cannot be manipulated (FI-NONCE-002). Invalid nonces are rejected — server rejects nonce=-1, 0, 999999999 (FI-NONCE-003). Nonce does not reset on reconnect (FI-NONCE-004).

howItWorks.title: Seed commitment integrity (5 tests)
howItWorks.content: Invalid seeds handled deterministically (FI-SEED-001). Seed is locked at bet acceptance (FI-SEED-002). Server seed unique per session — 152 distinct server seeds, 0 reuse (FI-SEED-003). No shared seed pool across users — 2 accounts, 50 concurrent rotations, 0 hash collisions (FI-SEED-004). Seeds not predictable from timing (FI-SEED-005).

howItWorks.title: Scope and limitations
howItWorks.content: This certification does not constitute a comprehensive security audit of the operator's platform, infrastructure, wallet systems, or application logic beyond the provably fair implementation. All test bets ($0.01 and $10) fall in bracket 0. Higher-stake bracket behavior is not verified.

pins.value: Cannot predict outcomes before betting — Pass
Results cannot be altered after bet — Pass
Each bet uses unique, sequential input — Pass
No hidden, mixed, or predictable entropy — Pass
No cross-round or cross-user state leakage — Pass
Parameters computed server-side, cannot be injected — Pass

sectionVerdictTable.finding: Outcomes cannot be predicted before betting

Commit-reveal protocol verified across 152 seed pairs

No hidden, mixed, or predictable entropy sources
No cross-round or cross-user state leakage

subsections.title: Purpose
subsections.content: This section indexes the evidence base for all 15 fairness integrity tests, maps each test to its verification source, and documents scope limitations.

subsections.title: Additional Game Integrity Evidence (S1-S4)
subsections.content: Properties verified in Sections 1-4 that provide assurance beyond the 15 FI tests:

rows: Client seed is a genuine HMAC input
Bet amount does not influence RNG

subsections.title: Scope & Limitations
subsections.content: This certification does not constitute a comprehensive security audit of the operator's platform, infrastructure, wallet systems, or application logic beyond the provably fair implementation. Explicit scope boundaries: Point-in-time: Certifies the implementation at time of testing. Post-audit changes are not covered. Bracket 0 only: All test bets ($0.01 and $10) fall in bracket 0. Higher-stake bracket behavior is not verified. No infrastructure testing: Server security, wallet systems, payment processing, and rakeback mechanisms are excluded.

extraCodes.label: API probe procedures · 6 tests
lines: # Send bet requests with nonce=-1, nonce=0, nonce=999999999
# Expected: server rejects or ignores client nonce value
# Send seed rotation with client_seed='' and client_seed=null
# Compare all server_seed_hashed pairs: 0/50 collisions = pass
# Check hex prefixes for correlation or pattern
# Send bet with rows=99, risk=extreme, rows=-1
# Send bet with extra payout_multiplier=1000 in request body

note.body: API probe results are inherently non-reproducible from static artifacts. They test live server behavior at the time of audit. The 9 data-driven tests (Steps 1-20) remain fully reproducible from the published dataset and open-source verification suite.

sections.title: Player Verification Guide
sections.subtitle: How any player can independently verify a game result
sections.intro: Every Plinko outcome can be independently reproduced using publicly disclosed inputs. No hidden variables, no private backend data — if your calculated slot matches the displayed result, the game was provably fair.

columns.heading: Key Principles
items: Every Plinko outcome can be independently reproduced
No hidden variables — no private backend data
If your calculated slot matches the displayed result, the game was provably fair
Most players can verify directly through the Duel UI

columns.heading: What You Need
items: Server Seed — revealed after seed rotation (casino entropy)
Nonce — the bet number in sequence (ensures uniqueness)
Rows & Risk — the configuration you played

walkthroughSteps.title: Play a Plinko Bet
walkthroughSteps.body: Choose your rows, risk level, and bet amount. The game generates a result using the provably fair algorithm.

walkthroughSteps.title: Open the Verify Tab
walkthroughSteps.body: Click on the bet result to open the details modal, then switch to the "Verify" tab. You'll see the server seed hash, client seed, and nonce.

walkthroughSteps.title: Rotate Your Seed
walkthroughSteps.body: To reveal the server seed, rotate to a new seed pair. The previous server seed is now disclosed and can be verified.

walkthroughSteps.title: Recompute & Confirm
walkthroughSteps.body: Use the disclosed inputs with the verifier (or run the HMAC-SHA256 yourself) to recompute the slot. If it matches, the game was provably fair.

sectionVerdictTable.check: Player can access all required inputs
sectionVerdictTable.finding: Server seed, client seed, nonce all exposed in Verify tab
4-step walkthrough verified end-to-end
Modal accessible from bet history on every bet
HMAC-SHA256 reproduced in JS and Python — 7,600/7,600 match
Standard crypto primitives only, no casino-specific black box

sections.verdictTitle: ✓ Any Player Can Reproduce Plinko Results
sections.verdictBody: Only disclosed inputs are used. Identical inputs always produce identical output.

subsections.title: Step 1: Play a Plinko Bet
subsections.content: Select your configuration (rows, risk level) and place a bet. The ball drops through the Plinko board and lands in a slot.

image.caption: Duel.com Plinko — 16 rows, high risk. Multiplier slots visible at the bottom.

subsections.title: Step 2: Open Bet Details
subsections.content: Click on the bet result in your bet history to open the details modal. You'll see the bet ID, slot result, multiplier, and payout amount.

image.caption: Bet #437825750 — Results tab showing 2× multiplier, Bucket 12 of 17, High risk, $0.10 wager, $0.20 payout.

subsections.title: Step 3: Switch to the Verify Tab
subsections.content: In the bet details modal, switch from "Results" to the "Verify" tab. This shows you the Server Seed Hash (SHA-256 commitment made before your bet), Client Seed (your player-controlled entropy input), and Nonce (the sequential bet counter for this seed pair).

image.caption: Verify tab — client seed, server seed (hashed), nonce, and "Rotate seed" button to reveal the server seed.

subsections.title: Step 4: Rotate Seed to Reveal
subsections.content: Click "Rotate Seed" to generate a new seed pair. This reveals the previous server seed in plaintext. You can now: (1) Hash the revealed server seed with SHA-256 — it must match the hash shown before your bet. (2) Use (serverSeed, clientSeed, nonce, rows) to recompute the slot — it must match the game result exactly.

image.caption: Verification result — seeds entered, Game Result shows "2x, Bucket 12 of 17", matching the live bet exactly.

subsections.title: Verification Complete
subsections.content: If the recomputed slot matches the live result, the bet was provably fair. The casino committed to the outcome before you bet, you contributed entropy via your client seed, and the result is deterministic from these inputs alone.

note.body: You don't need to trust the casino's verifier. The inputs are standard cryptographic primitives (HMAC-SHA256) that you can run in any programming language. The advanced section below shows exactly how.

subsections.title: Why Manual Verification Matters
subsections.content: True provably fair verification means you don't trust any casino-provided tool. Manual verification allows you to run calculations on your own machine, eliminate any possibility of a tampered verifier, and understand exactly how results are generated.

subsections.title: JavaScript Verification Script
subsections.content: Copy and run this in Node.js to verify any Plinko bet:

code.label: verify-plinko.js
lines: // Take first 4 bytes (8 hex chars), convert to uint32

subsections.title: Python Verification Script
subsections.content: The same verification in Python (no dependencies beyond standard library):

subsections.title: How the Algorithm Works (Plain English)
subsections.content: For each row of the Plinko board:
bullets: Take the first 4 bytes of the hash and convert to a number (0 to 4,294,967,295)
If even, the ball goes left. If odd, the ball goes right.
The final slot = total number of right bounces.
subsections.extraContent: A 16-row board means 16 independent decisions, each exactly 50/50. The ball path is fully determined by the three inputs and cannot be altered after the server seed is committed.

sections.title: Reproducibility & Artifacts
sections.subtitle: Clone, run, and verify every audit result yourself
sections.intro: This section consolidates the open-source repository, datasets, output artifacts, and reproducibility posture of the audit. Every finding in this report can be independently reproduced using the pinned commit, dataset, and commands below.

npm 8+
Git
TypeScript (installed via npm)
